Name: Exeggcute
Flavor-Text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Blazing sunlight has brought out the true form and powers of this Pokémon.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They usually live on ponds, but after an evening shower, they may appear on puddles in towns.

Name: Masquerain
Flavor-Text: It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction—to and fro and sideways.

Name: Maractus
Flavor-Text: With noises that could be mistaken for the rattles of maracas, it creates an upbeat rhythm, startling bird Pokémon and making them fly off in a hurry.

Name: Karrablast
Flavor-Text: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

Name: Phantump
Flavor-Text: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit’s rebirth as this Pokémon.

Name: Trevenant
Flavor-Text: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.

Name: Rowlet
Flavor-Text: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

Name: Rowlet
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

Name: Dartrix
Flavor-Text: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don’t groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.

Name: Decidueye ex
Effects: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that have damage on them.

Name: Grubbin
Flavor-Text: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.

Name: Fomantis
Flavor-Text: Fomantis hates having its naps interrupted. It fires off beams using energy it gathers by bathing in the sun.

Name: Lurantis
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By masquerading as a bug Pokémon, it lowers the guard of actual bug Pokémon lured in by a scent of sweet flowers. Its sickles bring them down.

Name: Morelull
Flavor-Text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

Name: Shiinotic
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

Name: Bounsweet
Flavor-Text: Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled-down fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in the past, when sweeteners were scarce.

Name: Steenee
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Steenee spreads a sweet scent that makes others feel invigorated. This same scent is popular for antiperspirants.

Name: Tsareena
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.

Name: Wimpod
Ability-Effect: During your first turn, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It’s nature’s cleaner—it eats anything and everything,  including garbage and rotten things. The ground near  its nest is always clean.

Name: Golisopod
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.

Name: Dhelmise ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

Name: Tapu Bulu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, it’s violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

Name: Arcanine
Flavor-Text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

Name: Alolan Marowak
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads. If at least 1 of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.

Name: Fletchinder
Flavor-Text: Fletchinder scatters embers in tall grass where bug Pokémon might be hiding and then catches them as they come leaping out.

Name: Talonflame
Flavor-Text: It has top-notch flying capabilities. It flies around easily, even while carrying prey that weighs more than 220 lbs.

Name: Litten
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won’t open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

Name: Litten
Flavor-Text: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won’t open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

Name: Torracat
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Torracat will let its Trainer coddle it once they’ve grown close, but it’s a powerful, sharp-clawed Pokémon, so its Trainer gets covered in scratches.

Name: Incineroar ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: Discard a random Energy from both Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped red nectar. It whips up blazing flames as it moves to the steps of its passionate dance.

Name: Salandit
Flavor-Text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Burned.
Flavor-Text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

Name: Alolan Sandshrew
Flavor-Text: Life on mountains covered with deep snow has granted this Pokémon a body of ice that’s as hard as steel.

Name: Alolan Sandslash
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 40 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses large, hooked claws to cut a path through deep snow as it runs. On snowy mountains, this Sandslash is faster than any other Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Vulpix
Effects: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After long years in the ever-snowcapped mountains of Alola, this Vulpix has gained power over ice.

Name: Alolan Ninetales
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A deity resides in the snowy mountains where this Pokémon lives. In ancient times, it was worshiped as that deity’s incarnation.

Name: Shellder
Flavor-Text: It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

Name: Lapras
Flavor-Text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

Name: Popplio
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

Name: Popplio
Flavor-Text: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

Name: Brionne
Flavor-Text: It gets excited when it sees a dance it doesn’t know. This hard worker practices diligently until it can learn that dance.

Name: Primarina
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from each of your [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To Primarina, every battle is a stage. It takes down its prey with beautiful singing and dancing.

Name: Crabominable ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Insatiable Striking attack does +40 damage.

Name: Wishiwashi
Effects: Put 1 random Wishiwashi or Wishiwashi ex from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Individually, they’re incredibly weak. It’s by gathering up into schools that they’re able to confront opponents.

Name: Wishiwashi ex
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Wishiwashi or Wishiwashi ex.

Name: Dewpider
Flavor-Text: It forms a water bubble at the rear of its body and then covers its head with it. Meeting another Dewpider means comparing water-bubble sizes.

Name: Araquanid
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokemon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It launches water bubbles with its legs, drowning prey within the bubbles. This Pokémon can then take its time to savor its meal.

Name: Pyukumuku
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 50 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

Name: Bruxish
Flavor-Text: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

Name: Tapu Fini
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This guardian deity of Poni Island manipulates water. Because it lives deep within a thick fog, it came to be both feared and revered.

Name: Pikachu
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Alolan Raichu ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Geodude
Flavor-Text: If you mistake it for a rock and step on it, it will headbutt you in anger. In addition to the pain, it will also zap you with a shock.

Name: Alolan Graveler
Flavor-Text: When two Graveler fight each other, it fills the surroundings with flashes of light and sound. People call it the “fireworks of the earth.”

Name: Alolan Golem
Effects: Discard 2 [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s grumpy and stubborn. If you upset it, it discharges electricity from the surface of its body and growls with a voice like thunder.

Name: Helioptile
Flavor-Text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

Name: Heliolisk
Flavor-Text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

Name: Charjabug
Flavor-Text: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

Name: Vikavolt
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.

Name: Oricorio
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar. It uses nimble steps to approach opponents, then knocks them out with electric punches.

Name: Togedemaru
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.

Name: Tapu Koko
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, if a person or Pokémon puts it in a bad mood, it will become a malevolent deity and attack.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

Name: Sableye
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

Name: Spoink
Effects: Take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Spoink will die if it stops bouncing. The pearl on its head amplifies its psychic powers.

Name: Grumpig
Flavor-Text: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

Name: Lunatone
Flavor-Text: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It’s active on the night of a full moon.

Name: Shuppet
Flavor-Text: It loves to feed on feelings like envy and malice. Its upright horn catches the emotions of people.

Name: Banette
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. It uses ethereal dance steps to call forth the spirits of the dead.

Name: Cutiefly
Flavor-Text: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. In fields of flowers, it gets into skirmishes with Butterfree over food.

Name: Ribombee
Flavor-Text: It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs’ effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.

Name: Comfey
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [P] Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
Flavor-Text: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won’t wither once they’re attached to a Comfey.

Name: Sandygast
Flavor-Text: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else—like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

Name: Palossand
Flavor-Text: The terrifying Palossand drags smaller Pokémon into its sandy body. Once its victims are trapped, it drains them of their vitality whenever it pleases.

Name: Mimikyu
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There was a scientist who peeked under Mimikyu’s old rag in the name of research. The scientist died of a mysterious disease.

Name: Tapu Lele
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It heals the wounds of people and Pokémon by sprinkling them with its sparkling scales. This guardian deity is worshiped on Akala.

Name: Cosmog
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn’t seem to care.

Name: Cosmoem
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −50 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: The king who ruled Alola in times of antiquity called it the “cocoon of the stars” and built an altar to worship it.

Name: Lunala ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [P] Energy from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Necrozma
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: It looks somehow pained as it rages around in search of light, which serves as its energy. It’s apparently from another world.

Name: Cubone
Flavor-Text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text: It grows stronger by enduring harsh training. It is a gutsy Pokémon that can withstand any attack.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.

Name: Solrock
Flavor-Text: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

Name: Drilbur
Flavor-Text: It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey.

Name: Timburr
Flavor-Text: It loves helping out with construction projects. It loves it so much that if rain causes work to halt, it swings its log around and throws a tantrum.

Name: Gurdurr
Flavor-Text: It shows off its muscles to Machoke and other Gurdurr. If it fails to measure up to the other Pokémon, it lies low for a little while.

Name: Conkeldurr
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Flavor-Text: Concrete mixed by Conkeldurr is much more durable than normal concrete, even when the compositions of the two materials are the same.

Name: Crabrawler
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon punches trees and eats the berries that drop down, training itself and getting food at the same time.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Put a random card that evolves from Rockruff from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

Name: Rockruff
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts.

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.

Name: Mudbray
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon covers itself in mud that it has regurgitated. The mud won’t dry out even if it’s exposed to the sun for a long time.

Name: Mudsdale
Effects: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

Name: Passimian ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move all [F] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Minior
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Places where Minior fall from the night sky are few and far between, with Alola being one of the precious few.

Name: Alolan Rattata
Flavor-Text: It shows no interest in anything that isn’t fresh. If you take it shopping with you, it will help you pick out ingredients.

Name: Alolan Raticate
Effects: Discard a random Item card from your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text: It has an incredibly greedy personality. Its nest is filled with so much food gathered by Rattata at its direction, it can’t possibly eat it all.

Name: Alolan Meowth
Flavor-Text: It’s accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. As a result, it’s very picky about food.

Name: Alolan Persian
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: The round face of Alolan Persian is considered to be a symbol of prosperity in the Alola region, so these Pokémon are very well cared for.

Name: Alolan Grimer
Flavor-Text: There are a hundred or so of them living in Alola’s waste-disposal site. They’re all hard workers who eat a lot of trash.

Name: Alolan Muk ex
Effects: 1 Special Condition from among Asleep, Burned, Confused, Paralyzed, and Poisoned is chosen at random, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. Any Special Conditions already affecting that Pokémon will not be chosen.

Name: Absol
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

Name: Trubbish
Flavor-Text: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

Name: Mareanie
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon wanders the seaside looking for food. It often gets electric shocks from broken Pincurchin spines that it tries to eat.

Name: Toxapex
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives within a dome made by its own legs. Toxapex monitors its surroundings by sensing the flow of the tide through its spikes.

Name: Alolan Diglett
Flavor-Text: The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon’s habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

Name: Alolan Dugtrio
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Their beautiful, metallic whiskers create a sort of protective helmet on their heads, and they also function as highly precise sensors.

Name: Excadrill
Flavor-Text: It’s not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill’s rampant digging.

Name: Escavalier
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

Name: Klefki
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Once it absorbs a key’s metal ions, it discards the key without a second thought. However, it will hang on to keys it favors for decades.

Name: Solgaleo ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Magearna
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has an Ability, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It synchronizes its consciousness with others to understand their feelings. This faculty makes it useful for taking care of people.

Name: Drampa
Effects: If any of your Benched Pokémon have damage on them, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

Name: Jangmo-o
Flavor-Text: They learn to fight by smashing their head scales together. The dueling strengthens both their skills and their spirits.

Name: Hakamo-o
Flavor-Text: The scaleless, scarred parts of its body are signs of its strength. It shows them off to defeated opponents.

Name: Kommo-o
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes +30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It clatters its tail scales to unnerve opponents. This Pokémon will battle only those who stand steadfast in the face of this display.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

Name: Skitty
Flavor-Text: It can’t stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

Name: Delcatty
Effects: Take a [C] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

Name: Fletchling
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is normally calm, but once it enters battle, its hormonal balance changes and it becomes aggressive.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [D] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

Name: Pikipek
Flavor-Text: It pecks at trees with its hard beak. You can get some idea of its mood or condition from the rhythm of its pecking.

Name: Trumbeak
Flavor-Text: It can bend the tip of its beak to produce over a hundred different cries at will.

Name: Toucannon
Flavor-Text: They smack beaks with others of their kind to communicate. The strength and number of hits tell each other how they feel.

Name: Yungoos
Flavor-Text: Its stomach fills most of its torso. It wanders the same path every day, searching for fresh food.

Name: Gumshoos
Flavor-Text: Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

Name: Bewear
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

Name: Oranguru
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 1 [C] more, and its Retreat Cost is 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: People used to mistake Oranguru for a human when they saw it issue command after command to the other Pokémon of the forest.

Name: Komala
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to it, it is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Komala spends its entire life sleeping. It feeds on leaves that contain a potent poison only Komala can break down.

Name: Big Malasada
Description: Heal 10 damage and remove a random Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.

Name: Fishing Net
Description: Put a random Basic [W] Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

Name: Rotom Dex
Description: Look at the top card of your deck. Then, you may shuffle your deck.

Name: Poison Barb
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Leaf Cape
Description: The [G] Pokémon this card is attached to gets +30 HP.

Name: Acerola
Description: Choose 1 of your Palossand or Mimikyu that has damage on it, and move 40 of its damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Ilima
Description: Put 1 of your [C] Pokémon that has damage on it into your hand.

Name: Kiawe
Description: Choose 1 of your Alolan Marowak or Turtonator. Take 2 [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon. Your turn ends.

Name: Guzma
Description: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Lana
Description: You can use this card only if you have Araquanid in play.Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

Name: Sophocles
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Alolan Golem, Vikavolt, or Togedemaru do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Mallow
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Shiinotic or Tsareena. If you do, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.

Name: Lillie
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Stage 2 Pokemon.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Blazing sunlight has brought out the true form and powers of this Pokémon.

Name: Morelull
Flavor-Text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

Name: Tsareena
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.

Name: Tapu Bulu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, it’s violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

Name: Alolan Marowak
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads. If at least 1 of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

Name: Alolan Vulpix
Effects: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After long years in the ever-snowcapped mountains of Alola, this Vulpix has gained power over ice.

Name: Pyukumuku
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 50 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

Name: Tapu Fini
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This guardian deity of Poni Island manipulates water. Because it lives deep within a thick fog, it came to be both feared and revered.

Name: Oricorio
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar. It uses nimble steps to approach opponents, then knocks them out with electric punches.

Name: Tapu Koko
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, if a person or Pokémon puts it in a bad mood, it will become a malevolent deity and attack.

Name: Cutiefly
Flavor-Text: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. In fields of flowers, it gets into skirmishes with Butterfree over food.

Name: Comfey
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [P] Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
Flavor-Text: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won’t wither once they’re attached to a Comfey.

Name: Sandygast
Flavor-Text: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else—like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

Name: Tapu Lele
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It heals the wounds of people and Pokémon by sprinkling them with its sparkling scales. This guardian deity is worshiped on Akala.

Name: Cosmog
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn’t seem to care.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Put a random card that evolves from Rockruff from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

Name: Mudsdale
Effects: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

Name: Minior
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Places where Minior fall from the night sky are few and far between, with Alola being one of the precious few.

Name: Magearna
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has an Ability, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It synchronizes its consciousness with others to understand their feelings. This faculty makes it useful for taking care of people.

Name: Drampa
Effects: If any of your Benched Pokémon have damage on them, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

Name: Pikipek
Flavor-Text: It pecks at trees with its hard beak. You can get some idea of its mood or condition from the rhythm of its pecking.

Name: Bewear
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

Name: Komala
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to it, it is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Komala spends its entire life sleeping. It feeds on leaves that contain a potent poison only Komala can break down.

Name: Decidueye ex
Effects: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that have damage on them.

Name: Dhelmise ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

Name: Incineroar ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Crabominable ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Insatiable Striking attack does +40 damage.

Name: Wishiwashi ex
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Wishiwashi or Wishiwashi ex.

Name: Alolan Raichu ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Lunala ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [P] Energy from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Passimian ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move all [F] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Muk ex
Effects: 1 Special Condition from among Asleep, Burned, Confused, Paralyzed, and Poisoned is chosen at random, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. Any Special Conditions already affecting that Pokémon will not be chosen.

Name: Solgaleo ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Acerola
Description: Choose 1 of your Palossand or Mimikyu that has damage on it, and move 40 of its damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Ilima
Description: Put 1 of your [C] Pokémon that has damage on it into your hand.

Name: Kiawe
Description: Choose 1 of your Alolan Marowak or Turtonator. Take 2 [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon. Your turn ends.

Name: Guzma
Description: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Lana
Description: You can use this card only if you have Araquanid in play.Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

Name: Sophocles
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Alolan Golem, Vikavolt, or Togedemaru do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Mallow
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Shiinotic or Tsareena. If you do, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.

Name: Lillie
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Stage 2 Pokemon.

Name: Decidueye ex
Effects: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that have damage on them.

Name: Dhelmise ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

Name: Incineroar ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Crabominable ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Insatiable Striking attack does +40 damage.

Name: Wishiwashi ex
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Wishiwashi or Wishiwashi ex.

Name: Alolan Raichu ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Lunala ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [P] Energy from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Passimian ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move all [F] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Muk ex
Effects: 1 Special Condition from among Asleep, Burned, Confused, Paralyzed, and Poisoned is chosen at random, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. Any Special Conditions already affecting that Pokémon will not be chosen.

Name: Solgaleo ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Guzma
Description: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Lillie
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Stage 2 Pokemon.

Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

Name: Ivysaur
Flavor-Text: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

Name: Venusaur
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

Name: Exeggcute
Flavor-Text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

Name: Squirtle
Flavor-Text: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

Name: Wartortle
Flavor-Text: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

Name: Blastoise
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

Name: Staryu
Flavor-Text: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

Name: Starmie
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Haunter
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Gengar
Effects: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

Name: Machoke
Flavor-Text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

Name: Machamp
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

Name: Cubone
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.
Flavor-Text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

Name: Marowak
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

Name: Jigglypuff
Flavor-Text: It hugely inflates its stomach and sings a mysterious melody. If you hear this melody, you’ll become sleepy right away.

Name: Wigglytuff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

Name: Venusaur ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

Name: Exeggutor ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

Name: Blastoise ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Gengar ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand.

Name: Marowak ex
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

Name: Wigglytuff ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Lunala ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [P] Energy from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Solgaleo ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
